result.stdout:
1.0.6-rc0004

A collection of utilities to interact with Azure

Usage: azureutilities [options] [command]

Options:
  -v|--version       Show version information
  -?|-h|--help       Show help information
  --ignore-defaults  Ignores defaults set using configure command and uses values passed to CLI

Commands:
  configure          Set defaults for the command
  deployments        Set of utility functions to manage the deployments in a resource group
  tags               Manage tags on resource group and resources

Run 'azureutilities [command] --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Set of utility functions to manage the deployments in a resource group

Usage: azureutilities deployments [options] [command]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help       Show help information
  --ignore-defaults  Ignores defaults set using configure command and uses values passed to CLI

Commands:
  clean              Clean deployment from deployment history from the resource groups
  count              Get the count of deployment currently in the resource group

Run 'deployments [command] --help' for more information about a command.

commands.payload:
Set of utility functions to manage the deployments in a resource group

Usage: azureutilities deployments [options] [command]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help       Show help information
  --ignore-defaults  Ignores defaults set using configure command and uses values passed to CLI

Commands:
  clean              Clean deployment from deployment history from the resource groups
  count              Get the count of deployment currently in the resource group

Run 'deployments [command] --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Clean deployment from deployment history from the resource groups

Usage: azureutilities deployments clean [options]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help                         Show help information
  -r|--resource-group                  Name of the resource group from which the deployments to be cleaned. You can specify this flag multiple times for multiple resource groups.
  -d|--days-to-keep <DAYS_TO_KEEP>     Anything older than this value here will be deleted. Default is 30 days.
  -ci|--client-id <CLIENT_ID>          Client Id
  -cs|--client-secret <CLIENT_SECRET>  Client Secret
  -t|--tenant-id <TENANT_ID>           Tenant Id
  -s|--subscription <NAME>             Subscription Id
  --ignore-defaults                    Ignores defaults set using configure command and uses values passed to CLI

Get the count of deployment currently in the resource group

Usage: azureutilities deployments count [options]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help                          Show help information
  -r|--resource-group <RESOURCE_GROUP>  Name of the resource group

  -s|--subscription <NAME>              Subscription Id

result.stdout:
Manage tags on resource group and resources

Usage: azureutilities tags [options] [command]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help       Show help information
  --ignore-defaults  Ignores defaults set using configure command and uses values passed to CLI

Commands:
  add                Adds the tags set to resource group or resources.
  clear              Clears the tags set to resource group or resources.
  list               Lists the tags set to resource group or resources

Run 'tags [command] --help' for more information about a command.

commands.payload:
Manage tags on resource group and resources

Usage: azureutilities tags [options] [command]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help       Show help information
  --ignore-defaults  Ignores defaults set using configure command and uses values passed to CLI

Commands:
  add                Adds the tags set to resource group or resources.
  clear              Clears the tags set to resource group or resources.
  list               Lists the tags set to resource group or resources

Run 'tags [command] --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Adds the tags set to resource group or resources.

Usage: azureutilities tags add [options]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help                          Show help information
  --tag <NAME,VALUE>                    Tags to add as Name,Value pair. You can supply multiple parameters like --tag Team,Marvel --tag Department,Infra
  -r|--resource-group <RESOURCE_GROUP>  Name of the resource group
  --filter <ODATA_FILTER>               Use OData query to filter the resources. For more info check $filter parameter from https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/resources/resources/listbyresourcegroup
  -ci|--client-id <CLIENT_ID>           Client Id
  -cs|--client-secret <CLIENT_SECRET>   Client Secret
  -t|--tenant-id <TENANT_ID>            Tenant Id
  -s|--subscription <NAME>              Subscription Id
  --ignore-defaults                     Ignores defaults set using configure command and uses values passed to CLI

commands.payload:
Adds the tags set to resource group or resources.

Usage: azureutilities tags add [options]

Options:
  -?|-h|--help                          Show help information
  --tag <NAME,VALUE>                    Tags to add as Name,Value pair. You can supply multiple parameters like --tag Team,Marvel --tag Department,Infra
  -r|--resource-group <RESOURCE_GROUP>  Name of the resource group
  --filter <ODATA_FILTER>               Use OData query to filter the resources. For more info check $filter parameter from https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/resources/resources/listbyresourcegroup
  -ci|--client-id <CLIENT_ID>           Client Id
  -cs|--client-secret <CLIENT_SECRET>   Client Secret
  -t|--tenant-id <TENANT_ID>            Tenant Id
  -s|--subscription <NAME>              Subscription Id
  --ignore-defaults                     Ignores defaults set using configure command and uses values passed to CLI
